commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `sessions.usage` so aggregate totals and breakdowns are computed from all matched sessions while only the returned `sessions` rows are capped by `limit`.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +53. Total +56 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main slices the discovered session list before loading usage and before aggregating totals, so a mocked or real store with more matched sessions than `limit` reproduces the under-count; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Wait for contributor-provided real behavior proof or a maintainer override; there is no narrow automated code repair needed from this review.

target.title: fix(gateway): compute sessions.usage aggregate totals from all sessions, not just the limited page (fixes #76496)
target.body:
## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `sessions.usage` aggregate totals (totalCost, totalTokens, etc.) were computed only from sessions within the `limit` page, silently excluding sessions beyond the limit. With >50 active sessions and default limit=50, daily usage reports under-counted by 15-60%.

- **Environment tested:** macOS, Node.js, OpenClaw repo at `f02a13e6`, TypeScript source with vitest.

- **Steps run after the patch:**
  1. Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/usage.sessions-usage.test.ts` — all 36 tests passed (18 × 2 shards).
  2. Ran `pnpm build` — built successfully in 77.6s.
  3. Verified new test covers the exact scenario: 3 sessions with distinct costs, limit=1, asserts aggregate totals include all 3.

- **Evidence after fix:**

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/usage.sessions-usage.test.ts
 ✓  gateway-client  (18 tests) 116ms
 ✓  gateway-methods  (18 tests) 116ms
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  36 passed (36)

$ grep -n "limitedKeys\|mergedEntries" src/gateway/server-methods/usage.ts | head -6
1150:    const limitedKeys = new Set(mergedEntries.slice(0, limit).map((e) => e.key));
1236:      { length: mergedEntries.length },
1247:    for (const [entryIndex, merged] of mergedEntries.entries()) {
1293:      const merged = mergedEntries[loaded.entryIndex];
1301:    for (const [entryIndex, merged] of mergedEntries.entries()) {
1437:      if (limitedKeys.has(merged.key)) {

$ pnpm build
✓ built in 506ms
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Aggregate totals now reflect all discovered sessions regardless of the `limit` parameter. The `sessions` array (per-session detail) is still capped by limit. All 36 tests pass including the new regression test.

- **What was not tested:** Live gateway with >50 real sessions. The fix was validated via unit tests with mocked session discovery.
target.author: liuhao1024
